question.label: Strategic bet gap
question.name: What stands between you and owning a strategic bet today?
question.description: Surfaces the main gap between his current role and the strategic ownership he wants.

answer: I should delegate more of the hands-on work to create room for that.

assessment.note: He names delegation of hands-on work as the main constraint, which shows a clear gap to strategic ownership with mild growth drag and capacity pressure. [THREAD-DEFERRED-WINDDOWN] 'delegate more of the hands-on work' is a concrete thread to pick up next session.

new_queue.label: Executor mode
new_queue.name: Where are you still operating more like an executor than a strategic owner?

new_queue.label: Next move
new_queue.name: What would your next move look like before we next meet?

issues: coverage: injected wellbeing into next question (0 touches after turn 7)

history.answer_excerpt: I should delegate more of the hands-on work to create room for that.
When conversations get political, I tend to retreat to what I can deliver directly.
I also have not been explicit enough about what opportunities I want.
I know I want bigger scope, but I still spend too much time in execution details.
I should delegate more of the hands-on work to create room for that.
I know I want bigger scope, but I still spend too much time in execution details.
When conversations get political, I tend to retreat to what I can deliver directly.
I can run projects, but shaping cross-org alignment is where I hesitate.
Part of it is confidence, I do not want to look unprepared in senior forums.
I also have not been explicit enough about what opportunities I want.
To grow, I need to own a strategic bet with visible stakeholders.
I should delegate more of the hands-on work to create room for that.
